answer:
**The *Gaṇapatitattva* (slug: `unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136`), a medieval Kawi-language Indic prose tantric dialogue between Gaṇapati and Īśvara (Śiva), details yogic disciplines in chapter 1 as the "Six-Limbed Yoga" (*ṣaḍaṅga-yoga*), comprising withdrawal (*pratyāhāra*), meditation (*dhyāna*), breath-control (*prāṇāyāma*), concentration (*dhāraṇā*), contemplation (*bhāvanā* or *tarka*), and absorption (*samādhi*).**[^1][^2]

**These practices unify the "Three Selves" (in-breath, out-breath, their union) into the Śiva-Self, leading to the Void.** The text connects them to ultimate liberation as the "Supreme Śiva" (*paramaśiva*): upon death or dissolution, the practitioner—via breath retention, mantra, and piercing the crown (12 finger-widths above)—transcends into the pure Void, becoming "the principle of Supreme Śiva."[^3][^4]

**Excerpts (verses 3–9 define the limbs; verse 48 culminates in supreme Śiva):**

> Withdrawal, and Meditation, Breath-Control, and Concentration,  
> Contemplation, and Absorption, are said to be the Six Limbs. // (3) //[^1]

Īśvara explains: "This is what is called the Six-Limbed Yoga... the yoga of withdrawal, the yoga of meditation, the yoga of breath-control, the yoga of concentration, the yoga of contemplation, and the yoga of absorption." Each is detailed sequentially (e.g., withdrawal restrains senses; breath-control pierces the crown).[^2]

> Liberated from the place twelve finger-widths above, he is the supreme Śiva.  
> The Void is proclaimed as supreme; liberation is to be known from that. // (48) //[^3]

"After one is liberated from the place twelve finger-widths above, the sacred Self properly becomes the principle of Supreme Śiva. It ceases to be the Self. What is its nature in that state? It is the pure Void, which is also the Supreme Void."[^4]

**Related practices include *prāṇāyāma* visualization of deities (Brahmā, Viṣṇu, Īśvara) and *yoganidrā* (yogic sleep via nose-tip gaze).**[^5]

[^1]: *Gaṇapatitattva*, ch. 1, [p-1a44bb](/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/01/translation/#p-1a44bb).
[^2]: *Gaṇapatitattva*, ch. 1, [p-12872f](/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/01/translation/#p-12872f).
[^3]: *Gaṇapatitattva*, ch. 1, [p-f36006](/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/01/translation/#p-f36006).
[^4]: *Gaṇapatitattva*, ch. 1, [p-875b7c](/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/01/translation/#p-875b7c).
[^5]: *Gaṇapatitattva*, ch. 1, [p-5f9128](/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/01/translation/#p-5f9128).
